events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario: Shaft alignment work observed at tested/inspected stage. Field condition: "alignment done before final pipe connection (pipe strain - SEQ/TRD)." This means alignment was performed before final piping was connected, which can introduce pipe strain after alignment is complete. API 686 requires that final alignment be checked/verified after all piping connections are made to ensure no pipe strain is introduced. Doing alignment before final pipe connection and not re-checking after would be non-compliant.

The work is through its testing/inspection step, but the alignment was done before final pipe connection - this means pipe strain could have been introduced after alignment, invalidating the alignment results. This is a defect/non-compliance.

Decision: fail (non-compliant)
Risk: medium-high - pipe strain can cause premature bearing/seal/coupling failure
Component conditions: installed-defective (the alignment state is defective due to pipe strain potential)
Percent complete: maybe 85-90% since alignment done but final verification missing
Confidence: high
